news.title: Dianna Russini still in shock after Mike Vrabel scandal and now has one thing on her mind... and it's not her husband!
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Dianna Russini still in shock after Mike Vrabel scandal and now has one thing on her mind... and it's not her husband!
news.content:
Fallout continues as former NFL insider shifts focus following career-altering controversy

The fallout from the Mike Vrabel controversy continues to ripple across the NFL landscape, and for Dianna Russini, the focus appears to have shifted dramatically. After stepping away from her role amid the storm, the former insider is reportedly concentrating on rebuilding her career rather than addressing the personal headlines surrounding her marriage.

The situation, which erupted after photos surfaced linking Russini and the New England Patriots head coach, has quickly evolved from a media scandal into a broader conversation about professional boundaries and personal fallout.

According to reports, Russini is now looking ahead rather than back, with her attention firmly on what comes next.

In the wake of the controversy, Russini has largely disappeared from public view, stepping back from both her professional duties and social media presence.

That silence has only fueled speculation, but reports suggest her priority is clear: rebuilding her career and navigating the aftermath of a situation that led to her resignation.

For a journalist who had established herself as one of the NFL's most connected insiders, the sudden shift marks a significant turning point. The focus is no longer on breaking news, but on managing the consequences of a story that placed her at the center of the headlines.

The controversy began when photos surfaced showing Russini and Mike Vrabel together at a resort in Arizona, sparking widespread speculation about their relationship.

Additional images later emerged from earlier years, intensifying scrutiny and raising questions about the nature of their connection. The situation quickly escalated, leading to internal reviews and ultimately Russini's decision to step away from her role.

Vrabel, meanwhile, also faced significant pressure, stepping away briefly from team duties to focus on family and seek counseling.

While both parties have addressed aspects of the situation, the broader impact has continued to unfold in the public eye.

As the story has grown, attention has increasingly shifted toward the personal lives of those involved. Russini's marriage has been pulled into the spotlight, despite no public statements directly addressing the situation from her or her husband.

The intense focus highlights how quickly personal matters can become public narratives in the modern sports media landscape, particularly when high-profile figures are involved.

At the same time, the lack of direct commentary from Russini has left room for speculation, with observers piecing together developments through reports and past appearances.

For now, Russini's path forward appears centered on professional recovery. Whether that involves a return to NFL reporting or a shift into a different role remains unclear, but the immediate priority seems to be stabilizing her position after a turbulent period.

news.title: Mike Vrabel's regret: He tries to win back wife Jen with a questionable gift
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Mike Vrabel's regret: He tries to win back wife Jen with a questionable gift
news.content:
Patriots coach makes sudden trip as fallout from controversy continues to grow

The fallout surrounding Mike Vrabel continues to take unexpected turns, and the latest development suggests the New England Patriots head coach is now trying to repair the damage away from the spotlight.

According to reports, Vrabel made a sudden trip during the NFL Draft to be closer to his wife, Jen, in what appears to be a personal attempt to steady a situation that has quickly spiraled.

The move comes after weeks of intense scrutiny following the emergence of photos linking Vrabel to NFL insider Dianna Russini, a story that has dominated headlines and fueled widespread reaction across the league.

In a week where his focus was expected to be entirely on football, Vrabel instead found himself navigating a deeply personal crisis.

Reports indicate that Vrabel left draft-related responsibilities to travel to Salt Lake City, where his wife was staying. The timing alone raised eyebrows, given how critical the draft process is for any NFL franchise, particularly one in transition.

During the trip, Vrabel was said to have purchased an expensive gift in what has been described as an effort to make amends. The gesture, however, has sparked mixed reactions, with some questioning whether it reflects genuine regret or a rushed attempt to repair public perception.

The situation has added another layer to an already complex narrative, where personal decisions are now intersecting with professional responsibilities.

The controversy began when photos surfaced showing Vrabel and Dianna Russini together, igniting speculation about the nature of their relationship. While neither party has publicly confirmed wrongdoing, the images were enough to trigger a wave of reaction across the NFL and media landscape.

Since then, the story has expanded rapidly. Russini stepped away from her role, while Vrabel addressed the situation internally, acknowledging that he had difficult conversations with those closest to him.

The resurfacing of older images and reports has only intensified the attention, ensuring the story remains firmly in the public eye.

For Vrabel, the timing could hardly be more challenging. The NFL Draft is one of the most important events of the year, shaping the future of a franchise and requiring full attention from coaching staff and executives.

Stepping away, even briefly, underscores the seriousness of the situation unfolding behind the scenes. It also highlights the human side of the story, where personal relationships are now being tested under intense public scrutiny.

The Patriots have publicly supported Vrabel during this period, framing the situation as a personal matter. However, the continued attention suggests the issue may not fade quickly.

While the immediate focus remains on his personal life, the long-term implications could extend into his professional future. Public perception, locker room dynamics and organizational stability all play a role in how situations like this are ultimately resolved.

news.title: All three England keepers played there - Carlisle's role in trio's rise
news.titleVi: [Dịch] All three England keepers played there - Carlisle's role in trio's rise
news.content:
Jordan Pickford made 18 senior appearances for Carlisle United, while James Trafford and Dean Henderson came through the club's academy.

Cumbria is an area well known for the Lake District, author Beatrix Potter and of course Cumberland sausages.

Turns out they make pretty good goalkeepers too.

All three of England's World Cup goalkeepers have featured for Carlisle United.

Dean Henderson and James Trafford both came through the club's academy, while the Three Lions' number one Jordan Pickford passed through as a first-team player on loan.

It's an accolade and source of huge pride for those presently and formerly associated to the club.

BBC Sport has been speaking to the coaches and scouts who played a part in the footballing journeys of the England stars.

Jordan Pickford made 18 appearances for Carlisle while on loan from Sunderland in 2014

Having made his England debut in 2017, Pickford helped the team reach the World Cup semi-finals the following year in Russia and has cemented his place as manager Thomas Tuchel's number one.

Now a veteran of the team with 85 caps at the age of 32, Carlisle played their part in his development after he joined the then-League One team on loan from Sunderland.

He only played 18 games for the Cumbria-based club but clearly left his mark.

"I remember one game in particular, we were playing at Deepdale against Preston," said Ben Benson, who went to the game as a fan but was also part of Carlisle's goalkeeping set-up.

"He made a mistake, one that goalkeepers up and down the country make every week."

The stakes couldn't have been higher in the incident Benson recalled as Pickford, aged just 19, had joined a club struggling to stay in the division.

Away to Preston he'd come for a cross and, despite seeming to have both hands on the ball, it fell from his grasp and Lee Holmes poked home to score North End's third goal in a 6-1 success.

news.title: Jude Bellingham reveals heartbreaking reason he gets emotional every time he sings God Save The King with England
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Jude Bellingham reveals heartbreaking reason he gets emotional every time he sings God Save The King with England
news.content:
By WILL PICKWORTH, SPORTS REPORTER

Published:  18:08 AEST, 21 June 2026   |  Updated:  18:34 AEST, 21 June 2026

Jude Bellingham has opened up on why he gets emotional every time he sings the national anthem before England games.

The Real Madrid star, 22, shone in the Three Lions' World Cup opener on Wednesday as they beat Croatia 4-2.

Bellingham scored a crucial goal at the start of the second half in what was his 49th cap for his country despite his tender age.

And, speaking ahead of Tuesday's clash with Ghana, Bellingham explained how much playing for England means to him and also gave an insight into the moment before matches when he sings God Save The King and how it makes him feel.

'I think the anthem is the last chance you get to really be mindful of the people who have got you there and what it means to be from England,' he said.

'I think about my grandad, who passed away just before my England debut. He was so patriotic.

Jude Bellingham has revealed why he gets emotional every time he sings God Save The King

Bellingham said he thinks about his late grandad and also the rest of his family (pictured with dad Mark (left), mum Denise (second right) and brother Jobe (right) in 2024)

'He was an Englishman through and through. He could give you every fact about every war, every battle, every king, and every queen.

'I do think about my grandad when that moment’s coming (just before a match during the anthem).'

Bellingham added: 'I also think about the rest of my family and the sacrifices they’ve made to get me to this stage.

'My mum, my dad and my brother, Jobe (who plays for Borussia Dortmund), have all been so important for me. My brother has been a constant crutch for me.'

This World Cup is the fourth major tournament Bellingham has represented England at and there have been several notable highlights along the way.

news.title: Revealed: World Cup referee who once complained about 'difficult' Cristiano Ronaldo set to officiate England's second group stage game against Ghana
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Revealed: World Cup referee who once complained about 'difficult' Cristiano Ronaldo set to officiate England's second group stage game against Ghana
news.content:
By TAMARA PRENN, SENIOR SPORTS REPORTER

Published:  18:37 AEST, 21 June 2026   |  Updated:  18:57 AEST, 21 June 2026

England's second Group L game with Ghana is set to be officiated by a referee who clashed with Cristiano Ronaldo while officiating in the Saudi Pro League.

Said Martinez from Honduras will have his compatriots Walter Lopez and Christian Ramirez as his first and second assistant referees, with China's Ning Ma as the fourth official.

It will be the second game of the World Cup for the 34-year-old, who took the reins when Qatar shocked Switzerland to pull off a 1-1 draw on the first matchday.

But Martinez will likely have little issue dealing with any potential histrionics from top stars in either England or Ghana's teams, having previously gone toe-to-toe with one of football's most notorious superstars on duty for his club.

Speaking to Mexican outlet Mediotiempo, via the Sun, Martinez admitted that the Portugal captain was 'very difficult due to his temperament'.

'He is a very competitive person, which makes him feel there's a lot of injustice towards him, so he complains a lot,' he added, recounting an experience he had refereeing one of Al-Nassr's games.

Said Martinez will officiate England's second group stage game against Ghana on Tuesday

'We disallowed two goals for him, with VAR and everything, but there was a play that upset him because he felt he was fouled in attack, leading to a goal against them. They were losing.

'He stayed 10 metres away from me and said: 'It's always the same with you lot, you always think you're the stars.

''But we don't bring you here to be the stars, we are the stars', in perfect Spanish.

'He started calmly but then got agitated and I just looked at him provocatively to see if he would get closer to me, but he didn't.'

Martinez shared that he discussed the incident again with Ronaldo in the tunnel, continuing: 'After checking the VAR, I asked him to come closer. Cristiano is Cristiano, and if he doesn't want to come, he won't.

'But he came over to where I was and I said to him: 'You're a great player, but what you said at half-time isn't right. I respect you a lot, but we're here to do our job just like you are'.

news.title: Stokes, Atkinson withdrawn from county matches as England returns loom
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Stokes, Atkinson withdrawn from county matches as England returns loom
news.content:
Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson look set to return to the England squad for third Test against New Zealand from Thursday after being withdrawn from their respective county matches by the ECB; watch the decisive final England vs New Zealand Test live from Thursday on Sky Sports

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player

Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have been withdrawn from the remainder of their respective County Championship matches in a clear indication that they will return to the England team for the final Test against New Zealand.

The ECB confirmed that both players had been withdrawn from their respective domestic matches ahead of the third day's play.

However, it is understood that the investigation into the nightclub incident involving both players is not yet complete. The squad for the concluding Test, which takes place at Trent Bridge from Thursday with the series level at 1-1, has also not yet been announced.

England captain Stokes and fast bowler Atkinson were ‌left out of the second Test against New Zealand after they breached the team's midnight curfew in the early hours of the Monday morning after victory in the first Test and were reportedly present during an incident involving a member of England's security staff and a Saracens player.

Both players were able to still play for their respective counties when the latest round of domestic fixtures got under way on Friday.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player

Stokes caught the eye with 95 runs against Northamptonshire on Saturday on a day England toiled in the fourth day of the second New Zealand Test, while Atkinson claimed 4-61 in Surrey's game at Glamorgan.

The ECB had the power to withdraw the players from those fixtures, which are due to finish on Monday, at any stage.

England homepage - news and views

Listen to the Sky Sports Cricket Podcast

Latest cricket videos and highlights

With the New Zealand series now tied at 1-1 after the visitors secured a crushing 253-run victory at The Oval, Pre-Test training for the Trent Bridge decider starts on Tuesday.

The five-day Trent Bridge Test is live on Sky Sports, with the first ball at 11am on Thursday.

news.title: Kohli set to return for India against England in ODI series
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Kohli set to return for India against England in ODI series
news.content:
Virat Kohli sustained hamstring injury in May's IPL final and has missed India's ODI series against Afghanistan; watch India's white-ball tour of England, from July 1-19, live on Sky Sports Cricket.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player

Virat Kohli is set to return to India's team for the One Day International series against England in July.

The 37-year-old legendary batter missed this month's ODI series against Afghanistan, which India won 3-0, due to injury but will make his return on India's tour of England for the three ODI matches between July 14-19, subject to passing fitness checks.

Kohli, the second-highest run scorer in international cricket history, sustained a hamstring injury during his Royal Challengers Bengaluru side's victory in the Indian Premier League final last month, when he hit an unbeaten 75.

Yashasvi Jaiswal has been left out of the squad to face England despite his century in the third match against Afghanistan on Saturday.

Bowler Harshit Rana is set to make his return from injury.

The three ODIs take place at Edgbaston on July 14, Sophia Gardens on July 16 and Lord's on July 19.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player

news.title: Henry extinguishes England's hopes inside an hour as New Zealand level series
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Henry extinguishes England's hopes inside an hour as New Zealand level series
news.content:
Sports Journalist
              @jacktwilkinson

Matt Henry's maiden 11-wicket haul delivered an emphatic 253-run victory for New Zealand as the tourists levelled the series with England inside an hour on day five at The Oval.

England resumed on 182-5 but saw their hopes of completing an improbable record run chase of 463 fade the moment Henry dismissed Joe Root (77) with his seventh delivery.

Henry (6-29) removed Jofra Archer, Matthew Fisher and Josh Tongue in back-to-back double-wicket maidens to leave England on the brink before he bowled Jordan Cox (25) to wrap up the victory and finish with 11 wickets in the match.

[Bản dịch tự động] Sports Journalist
              @jacktwilkinson

Matt Henry's maiden 11-wicket haul delivered an emphatic 253-run victory for New Zealand as the tourists levelled the series with England inside an hour on day five at The Oval.

England resumed on 182-5 but saw their hopes of completing an improbable record run chase of 463 fade the moment Henry dismissed Joe Root (77) with his seventh delivery.

Henry (6-29) removed Jofra Archer, Matthew Fisher and Josh Tongue in back-to-back double-wicket maidens to leave England on the brink before he bowled Jordan Cox (25) to wrap up the victory and finish with 11 wickets in the match.

news.title: Rice feels like he will get an assist at every corner
news.titleVi: [Dịch] Rice feels like he will get an assist at every corner
news.content:
Declan Rice has made 74 appearances for England

Declan Rice feels like he will get an assist "every time" he takes a corner - and says England supporters should be "excited" for what is to come at the World Cup.

England looked threatening from set-pieces during their 4-2 win over Croatia in their opening match of the tournament.

Harry Kane's opening goal from the penalty spot came after Noni Madueke was fouled following a Rice cross - and the England captain headed home his second from a Rice corner.

The 27-year-old was part of an Arsenal side that scored from a record 19 corners last season as they won their first Premier League title in 22 years.

However, the Gunners faced criticism for a pragmatic style of play and reliance on set-pieces (scoring 25 goals from set-plays overall).

"Everyone's enjoying [set-pieces] now, aren't they?" Rice said, speaking to BBC Sport at England's base in Kansas City. "Everyone's enjoying them now."

Rice became Arsenal's designated set-piece taker in January 2024 after a mid-season training camp in Dubai - and the Gunners are now renowned for their prowess from such situations.

"I wouldn't ever take corners or set-pieces, but [set-piece coach] Nico [Jover] and the manager at Arsenal saw something in me that others didn't," Rice said.

"They said that I can put balls in areas from a dead ball that no-one else can in the team at Arsenal other than Bukayo [Saka]. From that moment, I really just bought into that, believed in that.

"As time has gone on I feel like now every time I put down a ball for a set-piece - whether it's a corner or a wide free-kick - I feel like I'm going to get an assist or make something happen that's dangerous.

"That's a good mentality to have over set-pieces... and England fans can be excited."

The midfielder is England's vice-captain at this tournament and has become one of the most consistent corner takers in the Premier League.

England boss Thomas Tuchel has put an emphasis on wanting to play football that replicates the style that is played in the Premier League.

Rice says the Three Lions have worked on set-pieces since the German took over in January 2025 - but the tactical aspect of the game is what has dominated training sessions.
